name: Export Statistics by the Top 20 Countries in FY2024
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s export statistics for Fiscal Year 2024, focusing on the top 20 destination countries ranked by total export value for the full year 2024. It provides the total annual export value to each partner country, reflecting the geographical distribution of Cambodia’s export markets. The data supports analysis of export concentration, key trading partners, and overall trade patterns. Export values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Inter-Ministerial Prakas on Geographical Area Codes of the Kingdom of Cambodia 2025
description:
This Prakas is an Inter-Ministerial Prakas that shows the introduction of the capital, province, municipality, district, khan, commune, sangkat, and village identification codes of the Kingdom of Cambodia, as attached in the annex to this Inter-Ministerial Prakas. The Secretary-General, the Director-General of the General Department of Administration, the heads of departments and all units under the Ministry of Interior, the Directors-General of relevant General Departments, the heads of departments and all units under the Ministry of Land Management, Urban Planning and Construction, the governors of the capital, province, municipality, district, khan, and the commune/sangkat chiefs shall be responsible for implementing this Inter-Ministerial Prakas according to their respective duties from the date of signature. 
 This announcement was made in Phnom Penh and signed on September 9, 2025, by His Excellency Dr. Sar Sokha, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and His Excellency Say Samal, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Land Management, Urban Planning, and Construction.

organization.name_en: Ministry of Land Management, Urban Planning and Construction

name: List of Geographical Areas of the Kingdom of Cambodia 2023
description:
Cambodia’s geographical name list, first compiled in 1994 and published in 2009 with a second edition in 2019, reflects the country’s significant progress over the past four decades. Rapid development across national defense, the economy, and culture has improved social conditions and livelihoods, while changes in land management, urbanization, and administrative geography have created the need for an updated third edition in 2023.

This latest edition serves as a unified national reference for multiple sectors, including economic, social, cultural, and administrative functions, particularly land registration and allocation. It symbolizes the enduring benefits of peace as a foundation for sustainable national development.

The list standardizes geographical names in Khmer and includes Latin transliterations based on the Romanized System for Khmer to support international use. To strengthen consistency, the Royal Government has established a National Commission for Geographical Names to develop a unified national standard. This initiative aims to ensure consistency in geographical naming across Cambodia in both Khmer and Latin forms.

This pdf was signed in Phnom Penh on October 25th, 2023, by H.E. SAY Samal, Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Ministry of Land Management, Urban Planning and Construction.

name: Information Book of Capital-Provinces, Geography, Demographics, and Potential 2024
description: This pdf show the information such as capitals and provinces, maps, geography, demographics, and potential indicators in the Kingdom of Cambodia. It is an updated geographical area information in 2024 that had been signatured by H.E. Dr. SAR Sokha Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Ministry of Interior on 15th March 2024.​​ This book is designed to collect, compile, and present brief general information of the capital and provinces, such as geographical maps, demographics, and socio-economic potential, for sharing with all stakeholders to easily and quickly find information and data of the capital and provinces. This information and data can also be used as a basis for study, research, analyze, evaluate, and make decisions. It is compiled based on information provided by the 25 Capital-Provincial administrations as of October 2023 and official sources from other ministries and institutions. Although some information is still lacking and not yet comprehensive, this book provides benefits and knowledge for readers and information for the general public who are interested in studying and understanding the geographical, demographic, and socio-economic potential of each Capital-Provincial administration.

name: List Name and Statistics of Geographical Area of the Kingdom of Cambodia 2021
description:
The 1993 Constitution of Cambodia established Cambodia as a constitutional monarchy under a liberal, multi-party democracy and defined its territorial structure. Initially, the country was divided into provinces and municipalities, with further subdivisions into districts, communes, and sub-districts. Amendments to Articles 126 and 127 in 2008 reorganized this structure into capitals, provinces, municipalities, districts, communes, and sub-districts, with governance guided by the Law on Organization.

The reforms aimed to create a clear administrative system for urban and rural areas. Following the 2002 commune/Sangkat council elections and the 2009 sub-national council elections, Cambodia strengthened decentralized and deconcentrated governance. This restructuring was based on geographic, demographic, and socio-economic factors to better meet local needs.

Currently, Cambodia consists of 1 capital, 24 provinces, 27 municipalities, 162 districts, 14 khans, 1,405 communes, 241 sangkats, and 14,405 villages. These administrative records are maintained by the General Department of Administration Cambodia, reflecting ongoing adjustments aligned with national development.

This publication serves as an official reference for government officials, investors, students, and development partners, supporting research and socio-economic analysis. It was signed in Phnom Penh on February 26, 2021, by Samdech Kralahorm SAR Kheng, Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Ministry of Interior.

Moreover it also provide the definition of each budget content and code identification of each content indicator of budget. This Prakas was signatured by H.E. name: Cambodia Public Debt Statistical Bulletin Q4 FY2025
description: The Public Debt Statistical Bulletin FY2025 Q4 provides official statistics on the government’s public debt position and related debt transactions for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025. It presents information on total outstanding public debt as of the end of the reporting period, along with selected debt flows such as loan commitments, disbursements, and debt service payments. The bulletin may include breakdowns by debt type, creditor classification, creditor institution, and currency denomination. The data are compiled from official debt management records, may be subject to revision in subsequent publications, and are expressed in the unit specified in the bulletin.

name: Public Debt Situation Summary by Quarter FY2025
description: This dataset is compiled from the Public Debt Statistical Bulletin and presents a summary of the public debt situation by quarter for FY2025 (Q1–Q4), expressed in USD million. The dataset provides key public debt indicators, including debt stock, loan agreements signed, government securities, disbursements, and debt service payments.The data reflect both outstanding balances at the end of each quarter (stock) and financial activities occurring within each quarter (flows). Quarterly data are extracted from each respective Public Debt Statistical Bulletin. Revised figures presented in later bulletins are not used in order to maintain consistency across the time series.Technical notes Debt Stock including old debts(rescheduled debt and debt under negotiation). Debt Service Payment (External) including rescheduled old debts.

description: This dataset is compiled from the Cambodia Public Debt Statistical Bulletin and presents the quarterly stock of Cambodia’s public external debt for FY2024 (Q1–Q4), expressed in USD million. The data reflect the outstanding debt balance at the end of each quarter and are classified by currency (such as USD, EUR, JPY, and other currencies).Quarterly data are extracted from each respective Public Debt Statistical Bulletin. Revised figures presented in later bulletins are not used in order to maintain consistency across the time series.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2017
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2017, focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year. The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit. The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure. All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2016
description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2016, focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year.

The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit.

The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure.

All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Import Statistics by the Top 20 Countries in FY2017
description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s import statistics for Fiscal Year 2017, focusing on the top 20 partner countries based on total import value for the full year 2017. It reports the total annual value of goods imported from each partner country and reflects the geographical distribution of Cambodia’s import sources.

The dataset highlights Cambodia’s major trading partners on the import side and supports analysis of import concentration, supplier dependence, trade patterns, and international economic relationships.

Import values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Import Statistics for the Top 20 Commodities in FY2023
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s import statistics for Fiscal Year 2023 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 imported commodity chapters classified under the Harmonized System (HS) at the 2-digit level. It reports the total annual import value for each commodity chapter and ranks them based on their total import performance for the full year 2023 (Provisional). The dataset provides an overview of Cambodia’s major imported goods and highlights the structure of import demand across key product categories. It supports analysis of trade patterns, import concentration, sectoral dependence, and economic structure. Import values are measured in Thousand US Dollars (Thousand US$).

name: Export Statistics for the Top 20 Commodities in FY2023
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s export statistics for Fiscal Year 2023 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 export commodity chapters classified under the Harmonized System (HS) at the 2-digit level. The dataset reports the total export value for each commodity chapter and ranks them based on their total export performance for the full year 2023 (Provisional). The dataset includes commodity codes that are Harmonized System (HS) numbers used to categorize traded products and bilingual commodity descriptions (English and Khmer) to support both international and domestic users. Export values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Inter-Ministerial Prakas on the revision of the public service framework of the MLMUPC and procedures for the implementation of revenue and expenditure for the first systematic land registration of the land administration sub-sector program
description: This pdf file show inter-ministerial prakas on the revision of the public service framework of MLMUPC regarding to budget implementations for the first systematic land registration of the land administration sub-sector program. It also include the description, price, maximum duration, and expiry duration of public service adjustment and involved any related condition within. It was signatured by Minister of Ministry of Economy and Finance and Minister of Ministry of Land Management, Urban Planning and Construction on 23st July 2024. This prakas has main purpose to identify and adjustment on public service of land registration and budget implementation transparency, efficiency, and accountability of revenue-expenditure operations.

name: Inter-Ministerial Prakas on the adjustment of the public service framework of the Ministry of Mines and Energy
description: This pdf shows the adjustment of public service framework of Ministry of Mines and Energy involved and connected with any related legal document adjustments in energy and mines sector. It also illustrate both new public service and existing public service with unit price of service, maximum durations of service providing,  and expiry durations. Those fields of public service are classified by part, action part, and grouped by General Department under Ministry of Mines and Energy (MME). This prakas was signatured on 30th December 2025 by Minister of Ministry of Economy and Finance and Minister of Mines and Energy.

name: Public External Debt Loan Signed by Creditor Quarterly FY2024
description: This dataset is compiled from the Cambodia Public Debt Statistical Bulletin and presents the quarterly amounts of public external debt loan agreements signed by Cambodia for FY2024 (Q1–Q4), expressed in USD million. The data reflect the value of new loan commitments signed within each quarter and are classified by creditor (bilateral and multilateral, including creditor groups and individual institutions). Quarterly data are extracted from each respective Public Debt Statistical Bulletin. Revised figures presented in later bulletins are not used, in order to maintain consistency across the time series.

name: Law on Settlement of State General Budget Management FY2019
description: This pdf file show the law on the settlement of state's general budget management for fiscal year 2019. It includes, the budget settlement of national level budget, sub-national level budget, public investment by domestic and foreign financing. It also include, the balance sheet of main budget indicators by making comparison between budget amount in law of financial management versus budget amount implementation after settlement and budget modified law. It also include, the budget allocation through treasury and not through treasury.

name: Subnational Budget Summary in the Budget in Brief FY2026
description: This dataset presents subnational government budget information for Fiscal Year 2026 as reported in the Budget in Brief publication. The dataset includes approved revenue, expenditure, fiscal balance (surplus or deficit), and government support transfers to subnational administrations. The data covers subnational administrative levels, including provincial, municipal, district, and commune administrations. Budget figures are organized by budget group and detailed budget item categories as presented in the Budget in Brief. The dataset supports analysis of subnational fiscal structure, including revenue composition, expenditure allocation, fiscal balance, and intergovernmental transfers. All amounts are expressed in million Khmer Riel (KHR).

name: Cambodia Postal Code
description:
The Cambodia Postal Code dataset provides a comprehensive reference of Cambodia’s national postal codes organized by administrative hierarchy, including province, district, and commune levels.

Each record links a commune-level postal code with its corresponding district and province, with administrative names available in both Khmer (kh) and English (en).

The dataset supports address standardization, geospatial analysis, logistics and delivery services, e-commerce platforms, and government or statistical reporting. Postal codes follow a hierarchical structure, enabling analysis at provincial, district, or commune levels.

name: International Trade Performance Statistics of Cambodia, (2024 vs 2025)
description: This dataset, titled “International Trade Performance Statistics of Cambodia (2024 vs 2025)”, provides a summary of Cambodia’s trade performance over two consecutive years. It records total trade, imports, and exports, broken down by year, along with the trade flow type. Values are reported in USD, using the appropriate unit multiplier (e.g., billion USD) to standardize scale. This dataset enables comparison of Cambodia’s trade dynamics, highlighting growth or changes in imports and exports over the two years, and supports analysis of international trade trends.

name: Statistics of Cambodia Trade Promotion Policy Achievement, 2025
description: This dataset tracks the implementation and outcomes of Cambodia’s trade and export promotion policies. It captures programs, activities, and sub-activities related to policy implementation, formulation, intellectual property, trade promotion strategies, export facilitation, and exhibitions. For each activity, the dataset records measurable outputs such as export volumes, growth rates, MOUs signed, companies matched, market locations, and visitors, along with their unit of measurement and numeric value. The dataset provides a structured view of policy performance, supporting evaluation of trade facilitation, market diversification, and international business engagement.

name: Statistics of Domestic Commercial Activity Indicators, 2025
description: This dataset documents the programmatic activities of Cambodia’s domestic commerce and trade facilitation efforts, capturing both market promotion, user protection, and international trade support initiatives. It includes hierarchical program structures: program → activity → sub-activity, and records measurable outcomes (indicators) for each, such as number of contracts, participating families, purchase capacities, quantities of products, inspections, compliance observations, and established offices. The dataset uses unit scales to standardize measurement (e.g., tons, contracts, offices, USD) and allows monitoring of performance, operational outputs, and trade facilitation effectiveness across domestic and international initiatives.

name: Statistics of MoCs' Public Service Performance Indicator, 2025
description: This dataset captures the performance indicators of the Ministry of Commerce (MoC), including administrative, trade, and certification activities. It provides both English and Khmer descriptions for each indicator, the unit of measurement, the observed value, and, where applicable, the growth rate (%) compared to a prior period. The indicators cover intellectual property filings, business registration, permit issuance, certification services, and public service interactions, reflecting MoC’s operational outputs and service delivery performance over the reporting period. The data supports analysis of trends in business facilitation, trade services, and regulatory compliance.

description: This dataset provides monthly broker-level information on derivative trading activity at the Phnom Penh Derivative Exchange, including trading volume measured in contract lots and corresponding market share percentages. It identifies the licensed derivative brokers, the central counterparty responsible for clearing and settlement, and each broker’s operational status during the reporting period. Structured by reference year and month, the dataset supports analysis of market structure, broker participation, and trading concentration, and is suitable for regulatory monitoring, financial analysis, and market research on Cambodia’s derivatives market.

name: Solicited Projects
description:
This dataset contains information on Solicited Public-Private Partnership (PPP) Projects initiated by the Royal Government of Cambodia through relevant Implementing Agencies (IAs).
A Solicited Project refers to a PPP project proposed, prepared, and advocated by the Implementing Agency, which has been identified as having potential for development under the PPP mechanism.
Once approved by the Royal Government, the Implementing Agency issues a Request for Proposal (RFP) to select a qualified Private Partner in accordance with Chapter VI of the PPP Law.
The dataset provides details on government-initiated PPP projects, including the responsible agencies, project sectors, timelines, and current status, allowing users to analyze the government’s PPP pipeline, sectoral investment priorities, and infrastructure development trends.

name: Unsolicited Projects
description:
This dataset contains information on Unsolicited Public-Private Partnership (PPP) Projects proposed by private entities to Implementing Agencies (IA) in Cambodia.
An Unsolicited Proposal (USP) refers to a written proposal submitted by a private party without a government request, aimed at developing a new project under the PPP mechanism. Such projects typically introduce new concepts, technologies, or innovations that contribute to national development priorities.
The dataset provides details about project proposals, implementing agencies, sectors, and approval status, enabling analysis of private sector participation, innovation trends, and PPP pipeline development in Cambodia.

name: Cambodia NBC Bakong Transactions, 2022-2024
description:
This dataset presents annual statistics on transactions conducted through the Bakong system, Cambodia’s national retail payment infrastructure operated by the National Bank of Cambodia (NBC).
The data includes the number and total value of transactions processed in both Khmer Riel (KHR) and US Dollar (USD), providing insights into the growth and currency distribution of Bakong-based digital payments across years. The dataset was compiled from the NBC Payment System Annual Report , which documents the performance and development of Cambodia’s electronic payment systems.

description: This soft copy of the PDF presents the Announcement on Fiscal Rules and Procedures for Commercial Gambling Enterprises. It includes terminology definitions, the scope of regulations, and the list of registered tax obligations. The document details the methods and approaches for calculating total tax revenue from various gambling activities — including casinos, games of chance, and game competitions — as well as the monthly tax base calculation, tax credits on core inputs, income tax, and other fiscal duties. Moreover, it outlines the requirements for accounting and tax declarations, along with information on tax exemptions, sanctions, penalties, expiration, and implementation.

description: This dataset is the official list of Cambodian administrative divisions, detailing the hierarchy from Province down to the Village level. It was constructed by the Ministry of Land Management, Urban Planning and Construction (MLMUPC) and officially released after final adjustments and approval from the Ministry of Interior (MoI). Each record provides crucial data points including the unique official codes (e.g., '01020101) and corresponding names in both Khmer and English for the village, commune, district, and province. This standardized and authoritative geospatial reference is essential for government planning, resource allocation, accurate mapping, socio-economic research, and building standardized information systems across Cambodia.

name: Global GS1 Barcode Prefixes and Country/Region Assignments
description:
This dataset lists GS1 barcode prefixes (also called “country codes”) and their assigned Member Organizations or reserved uses. Prefixes may represent single codes, ranges, or special designations. 
📌: a prefix shows the GS1 Member Organization that issued it, not necessarily the product’s country of origin.
📌 :
- Prefixes not explicitly listed above are reserved by the GS1 Global Office for future use.

- 951 is used to issue General Manager Numbers for the EPC General Identifier (GID) scheme, as defined by the EPC Tag Data Standard.

- 960, 961, and 9620–9624 have been assigned to GS1 UK for GTIN-8 allocation.

- 9625 and 9626 have been assigned to GS1 Poland for GTIN-8 allocation.

- For 12-digit GTINs, and only 12-digit GTINs, there is an implied leading zero. For example, given the 12-digit GTIN 614141234561, the GS1 Prefix is 061, not 614.

name: Monthly salary tax brackets in Cambodia
description:
This dataset presents the official monthly salary tax brackets applied in Cambodia. It outlines the progressive tax system used to determine the amount of personal income tax payable by employees based on their monthly salary. The dataset includes income ranges (in Cambodian Riel – KHR), applicable tax rates, and quick deduction (excess amount) values that simplify the calculation of tax liabilities.
* Additional notes on application:
For resident employees: Salary tax is determined based on progressive rates according to the tax brackets below.

For non-resident taxpayers: Salary tax is withheld at a flat rate of 20% on all taxable salary payments made by the employer.

For additional monthly benefits: Employers must withhold salary tax at the rate of 20% on the total value of all fringe benefits provided to employees (including all applicable taxes). This withheld tax must be declared and paid together with the monthly salary tax.

description: The dataset provides detailed information on Cambodia’s import values by major trading partners, reported on a monthly basis. Each record includes the year, month number, and month abbreviation, which specify the time period of the transaction. The "country_partner" field identifies the partner country from which goods were imported, while the "value_thousand_usd" column reports the corresponding import value in thousands of U.S. dollars. This dataset enables analysis of import trends, partner country significance, and monthly trade dynamics, supporting research on Cambodia’s international trade performance and economic relations.

name: National Level Revenue Collections of Budget Settlement Law FY2005-FY2024
description:
This dataset presents detailed information on Cambodia’s national-level revenue performance under the Budget Settlement Law from fiscal year 2005 to 2024. It captures both the planned revenue (budget law amount) and the actual collected revenue (implemented amount), allowing assessment of fiscal execution and revenue credibility over time.
Revenue is structured hierarchically, beginning with main revenue indicators such as Current Revenue, Capital Revenue, Public Investment Financed by Foreign Countries, and Government Deposits. These are further disaggregated into sub-revenue indicators, including Domestic Capital Revenue, Grant Financing, Capital Revenue for Foreign-Financed Projects, Development Partner Budgetary Support Funds, and Government Deposits. Classifications are provided in both English and Khmer to ensure bilingual consistency.
The dataset also includes calculated deficit or surplus values, indicating whether actual revenue collection exceeded or fell short of budget pr

name: Budget in Brief of Capital-Province Level of Budget Settlement Law, FY2001 - FY2023
description: The dataset presents a comprehensive view of Cambodia’s subnational fiscal activities at capital and provincial levels, based on budget settlement law (implementation after the budget law is enacted). It covers three main categories: Revenue, Expenditure, and Budget Balance. Revenue includes Current Revenue (Tax and Non-Tax), national-level transfers (for capital-province and health functions), and Sub-National Fund support. Expenditure covers Current and Capital spending, while Budget Balance shows surplus or deficit. Reported in million KHR, the dataset spans over two decades, enabling analysis of fiscal capacity, reliance on transfers, spending priorities, and overall subnational financial sustainability.

name: Budget in Brief of Municipality-District Level of Budget Settlement Law, FY2013 - FY2023
description: The dataset presents an overview of fiscal activities at the municipality and district levels in Cambodia. It is organized into three main categories: Revenue, Expenditure, and Budget Balance, each with further sub-classifications. Revenue is detailed into Current Revenue (such as Non-Tax Revenue), Sponsor and Donation by National Level (including Sponsor for Municipality-District and Providing Education Function), and Sub-National Level Fund. Expenditure focuses on Current Expenditure, while Budget Balance records the Budget Deficit. All budget amounts are reported in million Khmer Riel (KHR). This dataset enables analysis of local government finance trends, including the scale of central government transfers, revenue generation capacity, expenditure needs, and resulting budgetary gaps across fiscal years, offering insights into subnational fiscal sustainability and policy planning.

name: Budget in Brief of Commune-Sangkat Level of Budget Settlement Law, FY2015 - FY2023
description: The dataset provides a concise overview of local fiscal operations captured from budget settlement law in Cambodia at the Sangkat and Commune level. It covers three main categories: Revenue, Expenditure, and Budget Balance, with further breakdowns into sub-categories such as Non-Tax Revenue, Sponsor and Donation by National Level, and Current Expenditure. Detailed classifications show budget flows from Sangkat under Capital, Sangkat under Municipality, and Communes, with amounts reported in million Khmer Riel (KHR). This dataset enables analysis of fiscal capacity, expenditure priorities, and budget deficits across years, highlighting the financial structure and resource allocation at subnational levels of government. It is valuable for assessing trends in local government finance and supporting evidence-based policy planning.

name: Expenditure Budget by Central Government Organizations of Budget Law, FY2002 - FY2025
description: This dataset presents annual expenditure allocations of Cambodia’s central government ministries and agencies. It records budget amounts in million Cambodian Riels (KHR), disaggregated by organization and detailed expenditure categories. Each entry includes the fiscal year, ministry name and abbreviation, high-level expenditure classification (Current Expenditure or Capital Expenditure), and sub-components such as Personnel Burden, Means and Services, Investment Expenditure, Financial Burden, Loan Disbursement, and Public Intervention. Covering more than two decades, the dataset provides valuable insights into government spending priorities, institutional resource distribution, and the evolution of fiscal policy. It is suitable for policy analysis, budget transparency, and public finance research.

organization.name_kh: ក្រសួងសេដ្ឋកិច្ច និងហិរញ្ញវត្ថុ

name: National Level Expenditure Operations of Budget Settlement Law FY2005-FY2024
description: This dataset provides comprehensive information on Cambodia’s national expenditure operations based on the Budget Settlement Law from fiscal year 2005 to 2024. It captures both planned allocations (budget law amount) and actual executed expenditures (implemented amount), measured in million riels, enabling assessment of budget performance over time through Treasury. Expenditure is classified hierarchically into level 1-Current Expenditure and Capital Expenditure, and further disaggregated into Level 2 categories such as Domestically Financed Public Investment and Loan Payment. All classifications are presented in both English and Khmer to ensure bilingual consistency. The dataset also includes calculated deficit or surplus values, reflecting differences between planned and executed amounts, along with remarks for contextual clarification.

name: Budget in Brief of Commune-Sangkat Level of Budget Law, FY2015 - FY2025
description: The dataset provides annual fiscal data on Cambodia’s local administrations at the Sangkat and commune levels. It is structured into three main categories: revenue, expenditure, and budget balance. Each category is further disaggregated into components such as sponsor and donation by the national level, current expenditure, and budget deficit, with more detailed breakdowns including revenue or expenditure from Sangkat under Capital, Municipalities, and Commune. The budget amounts are recorded in million Cambodian Riel (KHR), enabling trend analysis across years. This dataset offers valuable insights into fiscal flows between national and local governments, the financial capacity of Commune and Sangkat, and the balance between revenues and expenditures at the grassroots level, making it useful for policy evaluation, planning, and understanding decentralized governance.

name: Budget in Brief of Municipality-District Level of Budget Law, FY2013 - FY2025
description: The dataset provides annual fiscal data on Cambodia’s district and khan administrations over a thirteen-year period. It captures three main budget categories: revenue, expenditure, and budget balance, with further breakdowns into components such as current revenue, sponsorship and donation by the national level, sub-national level fund, current and capital expenditure, and budget deficit. Revenue is disaggregated into tax revenue and non-tax sources, while expenditures include both operational and capital spending as well as transfers to Sangkat under Municipalities. All figures are reported in million Cambodian Riel (KHR), allowing for trend analysis across years. The dataset is useful for examining fiscal flows at the district-khan level, assessing local financial capacity, and supporting evidence-based policymaking in the context of Cambodia’s decentralization and sub-national governance reforms

name: Total Expenditure by Capital and Province of Budget Law, FY2016 - FY2025
description: The dataset presents detailed information on Cambodia’s subnational budget allocations across all provinces and the capital city. It records annual data on the year, province code, province or capital name, abbreviation, and the breakdown of current expenditure, capital expenditure, and total expenditure in million Khmer Riels (KHR). Current expenditure includes personnel, services, transfers, and operational costs, while capital expenditure covers infrastructure, equipment, and investment projects. This dataset enables analysis of fiscal decentralization, expenditure trends, and regional budget priorities, offering valuable insights into how resources are distributed to support provincial development and governance.

description: The dataset records Cambodia’s budget allocation across provinces and the capital city from 1998 to 2025. It provides annual expenditure amounts in million Khmer Riel (KHR), classified by fiscal year, location, and expenditure categories. The two main categories are Current Expenditure (covering personnel burden, means and services, financial obligations, transfers, interventions, and other operational costs) and Capital Expenditure (including loan disbursement, property and equipment, and investment). With its combination of temporal, geographical, and financial detail, the dataset enables analysis of fiscal trends, spending priorities, and the distribution of resources across Cambodia’s subnational levels.

description: This dataset presents fiscal data from Cambodia's capital and provincial administrations of budget law for FY2001–FY2025, organized into three primary categories: (1) "Revenue" including current revenue streams and external support, (2) "Expenditure" covering operational and capital costs, and (3) "Budget Balance" containing surplus and deficit outcomes calculated from revenue-expenditure relationships. The data uses a multi-level indicator structure with varying detail depths, and all amounts are recorded in million Cambodian Riel (KHR) to enable consistent analysis of sub-national fiscal trends and policy impacts over the twenty-five-year period.

description: The dataset provides a detailed breakdown of Cambodia’s revenue structure as defined in the annual Budget Laws. Covering fiscal years 2019–2025, it classifies revenue into main indicators such as Current Revenue and Capital Revenue, further divided into sub-indicators including Tax Revenue, Non-Tax Revenue, and External Sources. Each category is organized by chapter, account, and sub-account, identifying specific revenue types such as income tax, VAT, property tax, and special VAT. For each year and classification, the dataset records the budgeted revenue amount in million Khmer Riel (KHR). This structure supports analysis of revenue composition, tax policy, and fiscal trends over time.

name: National Level Revenue of Budget Law FY1998
description:
This dataset presents detailed information on Cambodia’s national-level revenue structure under the Budget Law for Fiscal Year 1998. It provides a hierarchical classification of revenue, starting from main revenue indicators (Current Revenue and Capital Revenue), followed by revenue types such as Tax Revenue, Non-Tax Revenue, Domestic and Foreign Capital Revenue, and Capital Revenue from Loans.

The dataset further breaks down revenue into chapters, accounts, and sub-accounts in both English and Khmer, ensuring bilingual consistency and standardized fiscal reporting. Each record includes the approved budget law amount measured in million KHR.

This dataset supports fiscal analysis, public finance research, revenue composition assessment, and historical budget structure evaluation.

This information is valuable for policymakers, researchers, and businesses involved in the rice trade to monitor export performance and inform decision-making.

Higher-level indicators show totals of their sub-levels, while lower levels provide detailed budget items. All amounts are expressed in million KHR, giving a clear view of both overall and detailed fiscal allocations.

name: U.S. Tariff Rates to ASEAN Countries
description:
As of July 31, 2025, the United States implemented a new set of reciprocal tariff rates on imports from ASEAN member states, following President Trump’s trade directive aimed at reducing the U.S. trade deficit. These tariffs varied by country and were based on bilateral trade imbalances and negotiation outcomes.
Countries such as Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines successfully reached partial trade agreements with the U.S., resulting in moderate tariff rates of 19%–20%. In contrast, Laos and Myanmar, which lacked negotiated arrangements, faced maximum rates of 40%, reflecting their limited trade concessions. Brunei was assigned a 25% rate, while Cambodia received 19% under a separate designation. Singapore, which maintained a U.S. trade surplus, was subject only to the baseline 10% tariff.

name: Reciprocal Tariff Rates (Adjusted) – Further Modifications by the United States
description:
This dataset presents adjusted reciprocal tariff rates imposed by the United States on imported goods from various countries and territories. It reflects the further modifications made under U.S. trade policy measures aimed at responding to non-reciprocal or discriminatory tariff practices by trade partners. The adjustments are based on the principle of reciprocity—aligning tariffs with those that the partner countries impose on U.S. exports.
***Remark: In certain cases, it includes conditional adjustments such as:
“0%” for zero-rate response to high tariffs.“15% minus Column 1 Duty Rate” for dynamically adjusted rates based on existing duties

This dataset presents national government expenditure for Fiscal Year 2025 as reported in the Budget in Brief publication. The data is disaggregated by ministry and program, reflecting the approved budget allocations under the program-based budgeting framework at the national level.

The dataset includes ministry names in both Khmer and English, corresponding program codes, and the approved expenditure amounts for each program. The figures are derived from the Budget in Brief and represent summarized national-level budget allocations consistent with the approved annual budget.

All expenditure amounts are expressed in the unit specified in the Budget in Brief (million Khmer Riel).

name: Classifications of Activities, Products, and Sectors in Asian Countries 2018
description:
The designations and content presented in this publication do not reflect any opinion by the United Nations Secretariat regarding the legal status of any country, territory, city, or area, nor do they imply any position on the delineation of borders or boundaries. The data includes all countries in Asia.
1. Value added per capita (US dollars)
This column represents the average value added by the industrial sector per person in a given country, measured in U.S. dollars. It indicates the level of industrial output per capita and serves as a measure of productivity and industrial contribution at the individual level.
2. Share value added in GDP (%)
This column shows the percentage of a country’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) that is derived from value added by the industrial sector. It reflects the importance and contribution of industry to the overall economy of that country.

name: Expenditure by Sector of Budget Law in Brief FY2021 - FY2026
description: The dataset provides a structured overview of Cambodia’s national budget allocations as stipulated in the Budget Laws. Covering fiscal years 2021–2026, it organizes expenditure into a three-level classification: broad sectors (1. General Administration, 2. National Defence, Security, and Public Order, 3. Social Affairs, and 4. Economic), major categories (Current and Capital Expenditure), and specific components (Personnel Burden, Non-Personnel Burden, Domestically Financed Public Investment, and Foreign Financed Public Investment). For each year and category, it reports the budgeted amount in million Khmer Riel (KHR). The dataset supports analysis of fiscal priorities, sectoral trends, and the balance between current and capital expenditure, offering insights into the government’s budgetary structure and its implications for economic policy.

name: Cambodia Standard Classification of Occupations
description: The Cambodia Standard Classification of Occupations (CSCO) is a national framework used to classify and categorize occupations in Cambodia's labor market. Developed to provide a systematic and comprehensive classification of occupations, the CSCO enables the government, employers, and researchers to analyze and track workforce trends, employment patterns, and occupational standards. The classification is aligned with international labor standards and structured to reflect the diversity of the Cambodian economy, encompassing various sectors, industries, and skill levels. This helps in the development of labor policies, workforce planning, and facilitates efficient labor market research, ensuring a consistent approach to defining, categorizing, and measuring occupations across the country.

description: This table displays the percentage distribution of literacy status among internal migrants aged 7 years and older in Cambodia as of 2024, across different age groups. The data breaks down the proportion of illiterate vs. literate migrants, illustrating how literacy varies by age cohort. Younger migrants generally have much higher literacy rates (e.g. 9-14 years at 96.8% literate), while literacy drops considerably in the oldest age groups (e.g. 75+ years, 64.2% literate).

description: This table presents the proportion of households in Cambodia (urban and rural) that have access to toilet facilities and the types of these facilities in 2019 and 2024. It highlights notable improvements in sanitation, including increased toilet availability across both urban and rural areas. Additionally, the data provides insights into the types of toilets and connections — such as sewerage and septic tanks — that households use, as well as less common types like toilets built directly in water bodies or raised pans. The results underscore the progress and remaining gaps in improving sanitation infrastructure across the country.

The dataset also contains average household size, and categorical indicators showing whether a row corresponds to an area, region, or province. name: Distribution of School Attendance Among Population Aged 6 and Above by Sex, Age Group, and Residence
description: This table presents the school attendance status of Cambodia’s population aged 6 and above in 2019 and 2024. Across all age groups, the percentage of individuals currently attending school is higher in 2024 compared to 2019, especially in the youngest age group (6–11), where attendance rose from 90.6% to 94.1%. Notably, the share of individuals who never attended school decreased over time, reflecting expanded access to education. Among those aged 25 and above, school attendance remains very low, with over 80% reporting past attendance, indicating completion or dropout in earlier years.

name: Educational Attainment of Literate Population Aged 7 and Above, Cambodia 2019 and 2024
description: This figure illustrates the changes in educational attainment among Cambodia’s literate population aged 7 and older from 2019 to 2024. While the proportion of people with only primary or lower secondary education remains significant, there is noticeable progress in higher educational levels. The share of individuals with secondary/diploma education increased from 2.9% to 7.5%, and those with education beyond secondary rose from 2.8% to 4%, indicating a gradual improvement in access to and completion of higher education. Conversely, the share of those who didn’t complete primary school decreased, reflecting ongoing education reforms and outreach.

name: Adult Literacy Rate by Age and Sex Cambodia 2019 and 2024
description: This table presents the adult literacy rates in Cambodia for individuals aged 15 and above, disaggregated by sex and age groups for the years 2019 and 2024. Overall, the data shows consistently higher literacy rates among younger age groups and males. While literacy rates remain high for the 15–24 age groups, a slight decline is observed in the overall and female literacy rates in 2024, particularly among the 60+ age group. This may reflect ongoing generational shifts in education access and gender disparities in historical literacy attainment.

description: This table presents literacy trends among Cambodia’s population aged 7 years and older across urban and rural areas for the years 2019 and 2024. While Khmer remains the dominant language of literacy, the data reveals a growing share of individuals—especially urban males and females—becoming bilingual in Khmer and English or other languages. Between 2019 and 2024, Khmer-only literacy has declined, while multilingual literacy has risen, reflecting educational diversification and increasing exposure to foreign languages.

name: Age and Sex data evaluation Cambodia 2024
description: This table presents an evaluation of age data quality for Cambodia’s 2024 CIPS using three demographic indicators—Myer’s Index and Whipple Index (for ages ending in 0 and 5). The data is categorized by sex (both sexes, male, female). A lower Myer Index value indicates minimal age digit preference, implying higher data quality. Whipple Index values closer to 100 reflect accurate reporting, while higher values suggest heaping (overreporting) of ages ending in 0 or 5.

name: Distribution of Population by Sex Ratio by Broad Age Group Cambodia 2019 vs 2024
description: This dataset outlines the sex ratio (males per 100 females) by broad age group in Cambodia for the years 2019 and 2024. The sex ratio is above 100 in most younger age groups, indicating more males than females. However, the ratio declines with age, especially among the population aged 50 and above, where females outnumber males significantly. From 2019 to 2024, slight shifts occurred—most notably, the 30–34 age group saw a sharp rise in male proportion, while older age groups (especially 55+) showed gradual improvement in male longevity.

description: This dataset presents the distribution of Cambodia’s population by religion in 2019 and 2024 across different zones and regions. Buddhism remains the dominant religion, but the proportion of Muslims slightly increased between 2019 and 2024. The “Others” category notably increased in the Plateau & Mountains region due to tribal and minority religious groups.

name: Population Density by Regional Zone and Province Cambodia 2019 vs 2024
description: This chart shows the change in population density across Cambodian regions and provinces from 2019 to 2024. Phnom Penh remains by far the most densely populated area, increasing from 3,361 to 3,465 people per square kilometer. Other provinces, such as Kandal, Prey Veng, and Takeo, also saw notable increases in density, reflecting urban expansion and population concentration. Conversely, some coastal and plateau regions show relatively stable or low-density growth.

It highlights the population growth trends over time, with noticeable increases in urban areas and key provinces such as Phnom Penh, Prey Veng, and Takeo. Conversely, a few provinces like Preah Sihanouk and Oddar Meanchey show population declines between 2019 and 2024.

name: Number of Samples Distributed by Capital City and Provinces for CIPS 2024 Survey
description: The Cambodia Inter-Censal Population Survey 2024 (CIPS) was sampled in two different stages. The first-stage sampling units were enumeration areas (EAs), and the second-stage sampling units were households (HHs). A total of 750 sample EAs were selected according to the probability ratio relative to the size of households in a capital city and all provinces; furthermore, 22,500 households were selected as a second-stage sampling unit. After sampling the EAs, the household listing operation was carried out in all the selected EAs. The results of the household listing were used for the second phase of household selection. The second stage was to select 30 equal sample households according to Linear Systematic Sampling

description: The proportions of teen age marriage among men and women are nearly the same both 2019 and 2024 at the national level. However, in the urban areas there is a marginal increase that is matched by a marginal decrease in the rural areas. For both men and women, the proportion married among adolescents aged 15-19 in rural areas was higher than that in urban areas. The proportion ever married among women aged 20-24 in rural areas was almost double that in urban areas.

name: Historical Tax Revenue Collection by Main Business Activities of GDT by Monthly
description: The dataset presents monthly records of tax revenue collected by the General Department of Taxation (GDT) of Cambodia, broken down by major business activities. It contains the year and month of collection, the business activity category, and the corresponding tax revenue amount measured in billion Cambodian riel (KHR), with an additional field for comments or notes. Business activities include sectors such as import-export, financial services, agriculture, manufacturing, construction, real estate, transportation, telecommunication, education, and accommodation. This dataset supports detailed analysis of sectoral contributions to national tax revenue, allowing policymakers, researchers, and analysts to track revenue performance, identify trends, and assess the fiscal impact of different industries on Cambodia’s economy over time.

name: Historical Tax Revenue Collection by Category from GDT by Quarterly
description: This dataset provides a detailed record of Cambodia’s tax revenue collections by type on a quarterly basis. It includes the start and end dates of each quarter, the quarter identifier, and the tax category, such as Income Tax, Value Added Tax, Special Tax, Tax on Salary, Registration Tax, Withholding Tax, and others. The budget_revenue_amount_billion_khr column captures the amount of revenue collected in billions of Khmer Riel. The dataset allows analysis of temporal trends in tax revenue, comparison across tax types, and supports fiscal monitoring and planning by the General Department of Taxation from 2022 to 2025.

description: The dataset records quarterly tax revenue collected by the General Department of Taxation (GDT) of Cambodia, disaggregated by taxpayer categories. It includes the start and end dates of each quarter, the quarter designation (Q1–Q4), taxpayer groups (Large, Medium, Small, and Others), and the amount of tax revenue collected in billion Cambodian riel (KHR). This dataset enables analysis of fiscal performance, taxpayer contributions, and trends in revenue distribution across different taxpayer segments over time, supporting evidence-based monitoring of Cambodia’s tax system.

description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s macroeconomic outlook from 2019 to projected figures in 2026. It includes GDP growth rates by sector (agriculture, industry, services), inflation trends, and government fiscal indicators such as revenue, expenditure, and overall budget balance as a percentage of GDP. After a pandemic-induced contraction in 2020, the economy shows steady recovery, with GDP growth expected to reach 6.4% by 2026, while inflation and fiscal deficits remain relatively moderate.

The data includes counts of employees, micro-enterprises, and self-employed individuals who have joined the formal system, reflecting efforts to facilitate and monitor the transition of informal actors into the national economy.

name: The Working-Age Population That Moved Abroad to Live and Work for salaries by age group and gender(%)
description: The dataset provides detailed information on the percentage of Cambodia’s working-age population who have migrated abroad for paid employment. It breaks down the data by age groups ranging from 15–24 up to 65 and over, gender categories including male, female, and both sexes, as well as residential areas categorized as urban, rural, or national. This structure enables a comprehensive analysis of migration patterns, highlighting differences in mobility across age, gender, and geographic areas. The dataset is valuable for understanding which demographic groups are most likely to seek work abroad and how urban and rural populations differ in this regard, offering insights useful for policymakers and researchers interested in labor migration trends.

name: International Standard Industrial Classification of all Economic(ISIC) rev4
description:
This fourth revision of ISIC (ISIC, Rev.4) is the outcome of a review process that
spanned several years and involved contributions from many classifications experts and users around the world. This process resulted in an ISIC structure that is more detailed than the previous version, responding to the need to identify many new industries separately. This is especially applicable in the case of services. Moreover, the relevance of the Classification has been enhanced with the introduction of new high-level categories to better reflect current economic phenomena. A new section
entitled “Information and communication” (see part three, sect. J) is one such innovation. Comparability has also been enhanced with a number of other regional activity classifications, thus making this version of ISIC a much improved tool for international data comparison.

name: Cambodia’s Field of Science and Technology (FOS) Classification, 2024
description: The "Cambodia Field of Science and Technology (FOS) Classification, 2024" is a comprehensive framework developed by the National Institute of Statistics (NIS) under the Ministry of Planning. This classification system categorizes various scientific and technological disciplines to standardize data collection, analysis, and reporting across research and development sectors in Cambodia. It serves as a vital tool for policymakers, researchers, and institutions to effectively monitor and evaluate the progress of science, technology, and innovation within the country. The 2024 edition reflects Cambodia's commitment to advancing its National Science, Technology, and Innovation Policy 2020–2030, aiming to strengthen the STI foundation and ecosystem for sustainable and inclusive development.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2025
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2025 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year. The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit. The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure. All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Import Statistics by the Top 20 Countries in FY2025
description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s import statistics for Fiscal Year 2025 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 partner countries based on total import value for the full year 2025 (Provisional). It reports the total annual value of goods imported from each partner country and reflects the geographical distribution of Cambodia’s import sources.

The dataset highlights Cambodia’s major trading partners on the import side and supports analysis of import concentration, supplier dependence, trade patterns, and international economic relationships.

Import values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s import statistics for Fiscal Year 2025 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 imported commodity chapters classified under the Harmonized System (HS) at the 2-digit level. It reports the total annual import value for each commodity chapter and ranks them based on their total import performance for the full year 2025 (Provisional). The dataset provides an overview of Cambodia’s major imported goods and highlights the structure of import demand across key product categories. It supports analysis of trade patterns, import concentration, sectoral dependence, and economic structure. Import values are measured in Thousand US Dollars (Thousand US$).

in contribution payment.

name: Unemployment Rate by Age Group Area and gender(%) in Cambodia, 2019
description: This dataset presents the unemployment rate by age group, area, and sex. The table shows that the overall unemployment rate in 2019 was 1.2%, but generally decreased as the age range rose. Thus, it was highest for those who were 15–24 years old (2.5%), and lowest for those over 65 (0.3%). The rate of unemployment was highest for male youth (15–24 years) in the urban areas.

name: Unemployment Rate by Education Level Area and gender(%) in Cambodia, 2019
description: The data presented outlines the unemployment rates segmented by educational attainment, gender, and geographical area (urban vs. rural). It details percentages for different groups including both sexes, males, and females across three educational levels: those who never attended school, attended preschool, and completed primary and lower secondary education.

categories.name_kh: ប្រជាសាស្ត្រ និងសូចនាករសង្គម

name: Unemployed Population by Education Level Area and gender (%)
description: This dataset shows reflects the distribution of the unemployed in terms of their education levels. Of those who were unemployed in 2019, almost one-third (32.2%) had an upper secondary education, more than a quarter (26.7%) had a primary school education, about a quarter (24.8%) had stopped after completing the lower secondary level, and less than a tenth (6.7%) were university graduates. Among those with only a primary school education, there were more unemployed females than males. By contrast, more unemployed males had an upper secondary education or a university degree than their female counterparts. In the urban areas, a plurality (37.1%) of the unemployed had gone as far as the upper secondary level, while in the rural areas, equal numbers had a primary or upper secondary education (30.4% each).

name: Unemployed Population by Age Group and gender
description: In 2019, there were 97,687 unemployed persons aged 15 and above in Cambodia (Table 10.1). Among them, 38,879 were aged 15–24 (39.8%), 57,765 were aged 25–64 (59.1%), and the small remainder consisted of older persons (1,042, or 1.1%). In the youngest group (15–24 years old), there were more unemployed males than females, while the reverse was observed for older age groups (35–64 years).

name: Employed Youth by Occupation and gender in Cambodia, 2019
description: This dataset provides a detailed overview of employment distribution across various occupational categories, disaggregated by sex. It includes both the absolute number of individuals employed in each occupation and the percentage share of each occupation within the total employed population for males, females, and both sexes combined. The data covers a broad range of occupational groups, from managerial and professional roles to skilled trades, agriculture, machine operation, and elementary occupations. By examining this dataset, we can observe notable gender patterns in the labor force.

description: This dataset provides a comprehensive snapshot of youth employment statistics in a given population, disaggregated by sex (male, female) and location (urban, rural). The data focuses on the youth labour force, showing actual employment, unemployment, rates, and their share relative to the general population aged 15 and above.

description: This dataset show the overwhelming majority of employed persons were in informal employment, at 88.3% (89.0% for males, 87.6% for females).

name: Employed Population in the Informal and Formal Sectors
description: The dataset presents the employment distribution of individuals aged 15 and above across formal, informal, and household sectors by sex. Of the 7.9 million employed persons, 77.4% worked in the informal sector, 21.5% in the formal sector, and 1.1% in the household sector, with minimal gender differences. The majority were employed in the informal non-agricultural sector (males: 45.5%, females: 46.2%), followed by informal agriculture, and formal non-agriculture. A slightly higher proportion of females than males worked in the informal non-agricultural, formal non-agricultural, and household sectors.

description: This dataset contains information on the employed population categorized by education level, geographical area, and sex in Cambodia, 2019. It provides insights into employment distribution across different demographic and educational groups, allowing for analysis of workforce trends, gender disparities, and regional employment patterns.

name: Employed Population by Industry and gender
description:
This dataset show the industry with the highest share of the employed population was the services sector (39.8%). The share of the agriculture sector was 33.1%, and of the industry sector, 27.0%.
The same table indicates that one in three people in Cambodia were employed in agriculture, forestry and fishing, regardless of gender. However, gender disparities are apparent in other nonagricultural industries. Males were employed in greater numbers in construction (15.9%); transportation and storage (8.3%); and public administration and defense, compulsory social security (5.6%). On the other hand, females were more frequently employed in manufacturing (22.3%) and wholesale and retail trade (23.3%)

description: This dataset presents the annual percentage change in Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at constant 2000 prices, disaggregated by major economic sectors: agriculture, industry, services, and the total GDP. The use of constant prices allows for a more accurate analysis of real growth over time by removing the effects of inflation. The data starts in 1993 and shows how each sector contributes to the overall economic growth.

description: This dataset presents national government expenditure for Fiscal Year 2024 as reported in the Budget in Brief publication. The data is disaggregated by ministry and program, reflecting the approved budget allocations under the program-based budgeting framework at the national level. The dataset includes ministry names in both Khmer and English, corresponding program codes, and the approved expenditure amounts for each program. The figures are derived from the Budget in Brief and represent summarized national-level budget allocations consistent with the approved annual budget. All expenditure amounts are expressed in the unit specified in the Budget in Brief (million Khmer Riel).

name: Maternal and Child Nutrition in 2020
description: The dataset provides a structured view of monthly values across multiple provinces in Cambodia, 2020. It consists of four key variables: the name of the province, the corresponding month, the numeric value associated with each province-month combination, and a standardized date format to support chronological analysis. Each record reflects one observation for a specific province in a specific month, allowing for flexible analysis of regional and temporal patterns.

name: Cases of Referral Through the Health Referral System
description: In 2020, a total of 1,978,628 new disease cases were transferred from lower-level government hospitals to higher-level government hospitals. This included 83,663 cases transferred from health centers to referral hospitals and 1,897,370 cases referred either by mail or directly to the hospital. Among the 44,280 patients referred from lower-level health centers to higher-level health centers, 3,068 were transferred from health centers to referral hospitals, and 41,212 were referred from lower-level hospitals to higher-level hospitals.

name: Distribution of Public Health Centers by Province and Capital City
description:
By 2018, significant progress was made in health infrastructure, with public health facilities offering services through the Minimum Package of Activities (MPA) and the Complementary Package of Activities (CPA) at 1,457 locations:
1,205 Health Centers (1,137 MPA centers and 68 MPA centers with beds). 129 Health Posts (including 14 in provincial prisons). 123 Referral Hospitals (25 capital/provincial hospitals, 29 municipal/district/khan hospitals at CPA Level 2, and 59 at CPA Level 1). 9 National Hospitals providing Level 3 complementary activities and specialized services.

name: Cambodia Selected Indicators of Money and Prices (2008-2023)
description: The data illustrates key economic indicators from 2008 to 2023, including, forecasting year, annual consumer inflation rates, broad money and domestic credit relative to GDP, as well as nominal and real exchange rates. Inflation has fluctuated notably, peaking in 2008 and again in 2022, while broad money and private-sector credit as percentages of GDP have shown a clear upward trend, indicating increasing financial sector depth. Exchange rates have remained relatively stable over the years, with only slight nominal depreciation.

name: Vitamin A Supplementation for Disease Prevention in Children Aged 6-59 Months Nationwide in Cambodia Level, 2018
description: In 2018, children aged 6-59 months received Vitamin A supplementation to prevent diseases. In the first round (January-June), 1,289,779 children were supplemented, and in the second round (July-December), 1,260,648 children received the supplementation. Table 3.15 provides details on the number of children aged 6-59 months who received Vitamin A supplementation across provinces. Additionally, Vitamin A supplementation in the treatment of children included 17,973 cases of severe malnutrition, 2,030 cases of measles, and 3,606 cases of malnutrition/diarrhea/dysentery.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2024
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2024, focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year. The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit. The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure. All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Import Statistics by the Top 20 Countries in FY2024
description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s import statistics for Fiscal Year 2024, focusing on the top 20 partner countries based on total import value for the full year 2024. It reports the total annual value of goods imported from each partner country and reflects the geographical distribution of Cambodia’s import sources.

The dataset highlights Cambodia’s major trading partners on the import side and supports analysis of import concentration, supplier dependence, trade patterns, and international economic relationships.

Import values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Maternal and Child Nutrition in 2022
description: In 2022, maternal and child nutrition across Cambodia varied significantly by province, reflecting disparities in access to healthcare, dietary diversity, and socioeconomic conditions. Urban areas, such as Phnom Penh, demonstrated better outcomes due to improved health services and nutrition programs, while rural provinces, like Ratanakiri and Preah Vihear, faced higher rates of malnutrition and maternal anemia. Efforts to address these challenges included government and NGO initiatives focused on supplementation, breastfeeding promotion, and mobile health clinics in underserved regions.

name: Health Achievement Report - 2015
description: This national report presents Cambodia’s health sector achievements in 2015 and priority work directions for 2016. Prepared for the 37th Health Congress, it reviews results at the end of the Health Strategic Plan 2008 to 2015 and supports the transition to the Health Strategic Plan 2016 to 2020. The report covers four main areas such as reproductive, maternal, newborn, child health and nutrition communicable diseases non-communicable diseases and public health and health system strengthening. Topics include family planning, adolescent reproductive health, safe abortion, antenatal and postnatal care, safe delivery, child health, nutrition, HIV/AIDS, sexually transmitted infections, tuberculosis, malaria, disease surveillance, quarantine, mental health, oral health, eye care, cancer, cardiovascular disease, diabetes, injuries, road traffic accidents, violence, disaster management, environmental health, tobacco and health, service delivery, health financing, health equity funds, human resources, health information systems, governance, medicines, medical supplies, infrastructure, and private health services.

It reviews progress under the Health Strategic Plan 2016 to 2020 and examines major developments in health service delivery, reproductive health, maternal and newborn health, child health, immunization, nutrition, HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, malaria, non-communicable diseases, mental health, eye health, public health issues, health financing, financial risk protection, human resources for health, medicines and medical supplies, laboratory and blood services, health infrastructure, health information systems, regulation, private health services, governance, key achievements, main challenges, and priority actions for 2017 and beyond. The report is useful for understanding Cambodia’s national health system performance, policy direction, service coverage, quality improvement, equity, and planning priorities.

name: External Debt Outstanding in Asia, and Pacific Development Outlook, FY2015 - FY2020
description: This dataset provides annual information on the stock of external debt across Asian subregions and countries. It records the outstanding external debt (in million U.S. dollars) for each reporting year, disaggregated by subregion (such as Central Asia), individual country names, and ISO three-letter country codes. The dataset spans the period from 2015 to 2020 and captures country-level variations in external debt levels, enabling comparative analysis across time and regions. The data covers subnational administrative levels, including provincial, municipal, district, and commune administrations. Budget figures are organized by budget group and detailed budget item categories as presented in the Budget in Brief. The dataset supports analysis of subnational fiscal structure, including revenue composition, expenditure allocation, fiscal balance, and intergovernmental transfers. All amounts are expressed in million Khmer Riel (KHR).

description: This dataset details Cambodia's exports under Free Trade Agreements (FTA) and the Generalized System of Preferences–Most Favored Nation (GSP-MFN) scheme from January to September 2024. It breaks down monthly export values by key product categories, including garments, textiles, footwear, travel goods, bicycles, rice, agricultural goods, and other products. The data showcases fluctuations in export volumes, with a significant surge in June across nearly all categories.

name: Provincial Revenue Classified by Chapter of the Finance Law FY2020
description:
This dataset presents provincial-level revenue for Fiscal Year 2020 classified by chapter in accordance with the Finance Law (Law on Financial Management). The data reflects the legally approved revenue amounts under the official budget classification framework.

Revenue is disaggregated by revenue group, revenue item, and corresponding chapter code, consistent with the structure defined in the Finance Law. The dataset supports analysis of provincial revenue composition and budget structure.

All revenue amounts represent approved figures under the Finance Law for FY2020 and are expressed in the unit specified in the official budget document (million Khmer Riel).

name: Budgetary Central Government Expense of GFS, FY2023
description: The dataset details Cambodia’s monthly government expenditures classified under the Government Finance Statistics (GFS) framework. It records data by month, showing the total expense and its breakdown into key categories such as compensation of employees (wages, salaries, and benefits), use of goods and services, interest payments (to residents and nonresidents), subsidies, grants, social benefits (social security, assistance, and employer benefits), and other expenses. Covering diverse expenditure items, the dataset provides insights into government spending patterns, fiscal priorities, and resource allocation throughout 2023, serving as a valuable reference for macro-fiscal analysis and budget performance assessment.

description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s annual trade performance,
It summarizes the country’s total trade values, including domestic exports, re-exports, total exports, total imports, overall trade value, and trade balance.The dataset enables analysis of Cambodia’s trade growth, export structure, import dependency, and trade balance trends over time, providing useful insights for economic planning, policy evaluation, and international trade research.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2023
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2023 (Provisional), focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year. The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit. The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure. All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Public External Debt Service Payment by Creditor Quarterly FY2023
description: This dataset is compiled from the Cambodia Public Debt Statistical Bulletin and presents the quarterly debt service payments of Cambodia’s public external debt for FY2023 (Q1–Q4), expressed in USD million. The data reflect actual payments made within each quarter, including principal and interest, and are classified by creditor (bilateral and multilateral, including creditor groups and the names of lending countries or international organizations). Quarterly data are extracted from each respective Public Debt Statistical Bulletin. Revised figures presented in later bulletins are not used in order to maintain consistency across the time series.

description: This data demonstrates that the Royal Government of Cambodia received approximately over 12 million doses with the value of more than USD 86 million from major bilateral partners. China, in particular, remains the largest vaccine donor to Cambodia with 8.3 million doses equivalent to USD 58 million.

organization.name_en: Council for The Development of Cambodia

name: NGO Support to National Development by Sector
description: These datas show NGO funding by sectors from 2019 to 2021. The total NGO fund is about USD 365 million in averages per year over the last three years, of which around USD 100 million per annum was mobilized by NGOs from development partners. The data also identify that among 418 NGOs, the 30 largest NGOs contributed around USD 203 million, accounted for 57% of total support from NGO in 2020.

name: Disbursement by Sector (2013-2022)
description: Details trends in sectoral allocations of development assistance over the period from 2013 to 2022 which are well-aligned with the RGC’s priorities as articulated in the RS IV. Infrastructure sector received the largest share of ODA, around USD 760 million per annum, followed by economic and social sectors, which received supports around USD 375 million in average from 2019 to 2021.

description: In 2021, the percentages for women and men age 18-24 years who completed at least lower secondary school 52 percent and 47 percent respectively. The percentages for those who have at least completed lower secondary school are lower among the older population aged 25 years and above.

name: Percentage of population age 15+ with at least primary school completion in 2021
description: About half of persons aged 15 years and older have completed primary school. More men than women had at least completed primary school in 2021 and the gap between women and men has remained at 12-14 percent since 2015
