name: Reason for not attending school age 6-17 in 2021 (percentage)
description: The question on reasons for not attending school was asked only about persons below 18 years who were not attending school. Of this group in 2021, about 30 percent of women and 25 percent of men answered that they did not attend school because they had to work to contribute to household income. This first indicating reason was lower in 2021 than in 2019/20 for both women and men. The second most common reason they cited was that they don’t want to study, at 13 percent of women and 19 percent of men.

name: Percentage of population who never attend school by age group 2021
description: The percentage of the population aged 6 years and above who never attended school by age groups and sex. As indicated, the percentages of the population who never attended school has decreased in all age groups except age group 6-14 compared to last year. In 2021, more women than men never attended school among persons older than 24 years.

name: Percentage of population who attend school by education level 2021
description: The persons currently attending school by level of education and sex. There were large differences in participation rates between the different levels of education. In 2021, attendance rates at primary school were lower among women than men, at 54 percent and 58 percent, respectively. For preprimary education level, the attendance rates have decreased about 2 percent compare to last year for both women and men.

name: Literacy rate by age group 2021
description: In 2021, the literacy rates were highest in the age group of 15-24 years, at 96 percent and in contrast, it was lower in the oldest age group of 65+ years and about 61 percent only. The literacy rate for women is lower than literacy rate for men among persons older than 34 years. Literacy rates among women were lower than among men in the previous survey years.

description: The adult literacy rate is the share of the population aged 15 years and older who can both read and write a simple message in any language. The adult literacy rate for 2021 was estimated about 85 percent overall, 80 and 90 percent for women and men respectively. The adult literacy rate has increased with about percentage points since 2015.

name: Subnational Budget Summary in the Budget in Brief FY2021
description:
This dataset presents subnational government budget information for Fiscal Year 2021 as reported in the Budget in Brief publication. The dataset includes approved revenue, expenditure, fiscal balance (surplus or deficit), and government support transfers to subnational administrations.

The data covers subnational administrative levels, including provincial, municipal, district, and commune administrations. Budget figures are organized by budget group and detailed budget item categories as presented in the Budget in Brief.

The dataset supports analysis of subnational fiscal structure, including revenue composition, expenditure allocation, fiscal balance, and intergovernmental transfers.

All amounts are expressed in million Khmer Riel (KHR), consistent with the Budget in Brief FY2021.

The dataset includes ministry names in both Khmer and English, corresponding program codes, and the approved expenditure amounts for each program. All expenditure amounts are expressed in the unit specified in the Budget in Brief (million Khmer Riel).

name: Cambodia Public Debt Statistical Bulletin Q4 FY2023
description: The Public Debt Statistical Bulletin FY2023 Q4 provides official statistics on the government’s public debt position and related debt transactions for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2023. It presents information on total outstanding public debt as of the end of the reporting period, along with selected debt flows such as loan commitments, disbursements, and debt service payments. The bulletin may include breakdowns by debt type, creditor classification, creditor institution, and currency denomination. The data are compiled from official debt management records, may be subject to revision in subsequent publications, and are expressed in the unit specified in the bulletin.

name: Export Statistics for the Top 20 Commodities in FY2018
description:
This dataset presents Cambodia’s export statistics for Fiscal Year 2018, focusing on the top 20 export commodity chapters classified under the Harmonized System (HS) at the 2-digit level. The dataset reports the total export value for each commodity chapter and ranks them based on their total export performance for the full year 2018. 

The dataset includes commodity codes that are Harmonized System (HS) numbers used to categorize traded products and bilingual commodity descriptions (English and Khmer) to support both international and domestic users. Export values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Trade Balance Statistics by Top 20 Countries in FY2018
description: This dataset presents Cambodia’s trade balance statistics for Fiscal Year 2018, focusing on the top 20 partner countries. It provides information on the total export value, total import value, and the resulting trade balance with each partner country for the full year. The trade balance is calculated as the difference between exports and imports by exports minus imports. A positive value indicates a trade surplus, while a negative value indicates a trade deficit. The dataset highlights Cambodia’s bilateral trade performance and supports analysis of trade surplus/deficit patterns, partner country dependence, and overall external trade structure. All monetary values are measured in Thousand US Dollars.

name: Cambodia Inflation from 2005 to 2025
description: Inflation measures the rate at which the general price level for goods and services rises, affecting the purchasing power of currency. This dataset tracks inflation in Cambodia using the Consumer Price Index (CPI) as the primary metric. It contains monthly historical data including raw CPI Index values, Month-on-Month (MoM) and Year-on-Year (YoY) percentage changes and rolling 3-month and 12-month moving averages. These metrics are designed to smooth out volatility and identify long-term price trends for macroeconomic analysis and fiscal planning.

name: Cambodia Gross Domestic Product​ (GDP) at Constant 2000 prices from 2005-2024
description: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at constant 2000 price is a measure of a country's economic output that accounts for inflation. It reflects the value of all goods and services produced within a country in a given year, adjusted to the price levels of the year 2000. This adjustment allows for comparison of economic productivity across different years without the distortion caused by inflation or deflation.

name: Cambodia Monetary Survey from 1993-2019
description: The Monetary Survey provides a detailed overview of the financial sector's status by aggregating data from central banks, commercial banks, and other financial institutions. It includes key metrics such as monetary aggregates, credit distribution, and overall money supply. This survey is essential for evaluating the effectiveness of monetary policies, understanding money flow within the economy, and supporting economic analysis and policy-making. There are three main indicator of monetary survey including, Net Domestic Assets, Liquidity, and Net Foreign Assets.

name: Budget in Brief of Central Government, FY2005 - FY2024
description: This dataset presents annual budgetary information of the central government in million Cambodian Riels (KHR) captured from budge law. It records revenues, expenditures, capital formation, fiscal balances, and financing activities, organized into two hierarchical levels of indicators. The first level classifies the budget into major categories such as Revenue, Expense, Net Acquisition of Nonfinancial Assets, Gross Operating Balance, and Financing, while the second level provides detailed components such as Taxes, Grants, Compensation of employees, Fixed assets, and Domestic borrowing. This structure enables comprehensive analysis of Cambodia’s fiscal performance, including revenue mobilization, expenditure priorities, deficit or surplus trends, and financing methods over nearly two decades. The dataset is well-suited for fiscal policy analysis, macroeconomic research, visualization in BI tools, and monitoring government financial management practices.
